Preface Welcome! In the spring of 2018, I did my internship “creation of new product”". I was asked to do a market study. This report contains all my work done during this part of time. This guide is also available in pdf format. Some information is not available because of confidentiality.

Acknowledgments Before developing this professional experience, it seems important to me to start this technical report by thanking the people without whom this internship could not have taken place. First of all, I thank Mr. Jean-Christophe Lallemand, without whom this intership would never have taken place. Then, I thank Ms. Anaïs Hoareau to have participated in the supervision of it. Finally, I thank Mr. Sacha Vincent to have participated in this project.

Introduction This internship “creation of new product” is a 5 weeks internship. It has been done at the start-up IWANT, leaded by Anaïs Hoareau. During this time, a market study has been done as well as 3 business plans and the functional specifications. The logo and some advertising banners has been created too. Some information aren’t in this document because of confidentiality. The strat-up was at its beginning. Its main goal was to create an application. The aim of this internship was not to create it. It was to study if this application would be used or not, so to know if this project should continue.

Chapter 1 Functional specifications In order to create the application, it is important to define the functional specifications. The document about it contains 28 pages. It is based on 2 main functions and 46 secondary ones. Among the secondary functions, there are create a profil and modify it. There were also 5 esteem functions like having an easy-to-use interface, 6 constraint ones such as compatible with Adroid and IOS and 6 technical ones: multilingual for example. The usage cases have been studied too. Below, there is the example for the creation of profile. The challenges of sustainable development has been gone through. More than 18 of them has been taken into account. Here are some examples of it: To take into account the specificities of each person as an employee and/or customer To optimise transport during business trips In the next part, the business plans will be explained.

Chapter 2 Business plans There are 3 business plans available. They are on 3 years. The first one is for little costs, the second one is for moderate costs and the last one is for a quick expansion in Europe. The three cases of common points. They are: Company structure: SAS No salary in the first year, hence the need for partners Same target cities for starting, prospecting and setting up partnerships during the first 6 months Contribution of 15,000 euros 20,000€ acquired from an investor Launch at month 6 For the case of little costs: 3 partners at the beginning: Founder, Community Manager, Engineer developer Design of the application, site and internal maintenance No purchase of computers (personal computers) No recruitment Year 3: Loft rental For the case of moderate costs: 2 partners at the beginning: Founder, Community Manager External design and maintenance (application: 18 000€, site: 18 000€, maintenance: 4500€) Year 1: Loan of 25 000€ Year 2: Recruitment engineer developer, rental of a loft, purchase 3 computers (3 000€) Year 3: Recruitment of a prospecting agent For the case of quick expansion in Europe: 2 associés au départ : Fondatrice, Community Manager Conception et maintenance externe (application: 18 000€, site: 18 000€, maintenance 4500€; HT) Année 1 : Prêt de 25 000€ Année 2 : Location d’un loft, achat de 4 ordinateurs (4 000€), recrutement d’un ingénieur développeur et d’un agent de prospection internationale Année 3 : achat de 2 ordinateurs (2 000€), recrutement de 2 agents de prospection internationale Some hypothesis have been done. One of these is 70% of research generates money and each user do 3 searches per week. Another one is the advertising banner in the application generates 0.13€ for 1000 views. To be profitable at the end of the first year, 30000 users are needed for the little costs case. For the two others, 55000 are needed. At the end of the third year, 40000 are needed for the little costs case, 64000 for the moderate costs one and 85000 for the quick expansion one. This seems realistic according to the number of people living to the cities where the application will be used. Indeed, it represents a little proportion of them. In order to know if the project is realistic, a market study has been done. In the next part, it will be introduced.

Chapter 3 Market study This market study as been divided in two parts. The first one is the analysis of the competition and the other one is a sidewalk. In the first part, analysis of the competition has been done. It used an Excel file. For each rows, a company was analyzed. Each column corresponds to a research topic. Some topics were our advantage compare to them, their principal functions and what are their main assets for the customer. At the end of the study, 63 competitors have been studied during the analysis. Then a sidewalk has been prepared. It took place in Marseille. In 3 days, we had 50 answers to our survey from companies, 100 answers from people on the sidewalk and 100 others from a google form send to students from the main cities of France. There were 3 questionnaires, one for each survey. For examples, some questions asked were: What is your sector of activity? How much do you tolerate advertising on an application? The questionnaire from the google form is very similar to the one for the sidewalk. The only difference was a question in order to know where they are from. Knowing that people are interested by the project, the next stage was to begin the advertisement in order to attract new users.

Chapter 4 Logo and advertising banners The logo is a W because of the name IWANT. Its color is one of the application. Around it there is a white square like a painting frame. Below there is the logo: Below, there is an example of sticker that can be given to the partner companies. At the bottom of it, it is written: “You can download this app by scanning the QR code present above”. In order to be known by the students, some cards can be given to them. They are similar to the sticker. There is also the possibility to display a poster on a bus, like this one: The advertisement on bus station has been studied too. It will contains a slogan for the application. But this slogan is confidential.

Conclusion This internship has been the opportunity to work on a project carry on by another student of my school, Anaïs Hoareau. I learn the important steps of launching a new product. The main result is that the project can carry on. Indeed, people were interested by this project. The sidewalk allowed me to improve my social skills. It also showed me that people are more receptive when they are relaxing in a park or waiting for a train. In those situation, they always agreed to respond to the survey.

Abstract During this internship, I discovered how to work in a start-up. I also participated to a sidewalk which was a part of a market study. This market study include an analysis of the competition. I improved my marketing skill by creating logo and advertising banners. Moreover, 3 business plans have been created. Keywords: Start-up, business plan, market study, marketing
